why: Canonical snake_case (D2): static capability mismatch — retry to the consumer's configured max_deliver, then dead-letter.
Canonical snake_case (D2): sovereignty/policy refusal — capable but declines; retry-then-dead-letter.
Canonical snake_case (D2): transient load — exponential backoff, excluded from the exhaustion budget (§4.1, §5.1).
Canonical snake_case (D2): M7 attestation refusal — immediate dead-letter fast path, never retried.
Kebab 'cant-do' is a receive-window alias of the snake canonical (§3.4); normalization runs BEFORE the unknown-coerce, or every live alias misroutes mid-window (grill D5). Retires at flag-day R.
Kebab 'wont-do' is a receive-window alias of the snake canonical (§3.4); normalization runs BEFORE the unknown-coerce, or every live alias misroutes mid-window (grill D5). Retires at flag-day R.
Kebab 'not-now' is a receive-window alias of the snake canonical (§3.4); normalization runs BEFORE the unknown-coerce, or every live alias misroutes mid-window (grill D5). Retires at flag-day R.

Kebab 'compliance-block' is a receive-window alias of the snake canonical (§3.4); normalization runs BEFORE the unknown-coerce, or every live alias misroutes mid-window (grill D5). Retires at flag-day R.

Not a transport token — a pre-spawn authorization refusal homed in RFC-0010's kind registry (grill D4, §3.4). Unknown after normalization, so the transport reads it as cant_do; the cortex fifth-value emission is a conformance defect fixed at flag-day R.
Genuinely unknown after normalization → coerce to cant_do (§3.4 MUST, grill D5). Closed-for-emit, tolerant-for-receive.
Missing/empty reason coerces to cant_do (§3.4 MUST) — the formerly-orphaned f-022 fallback, now normative and enforced by this vector.

THE LAYERED CARVE (§3, grill D24): disposition routes off the 0007 token; retry_after_ms from the 0010 refusal object overrides the backoff curve RAW (§4.1, no clamp — grill D6); object grammar and token-object consistency are RFC-0010's to adjudicate.
No refusal object present → the §4.1 delivery_count curve supplies the delay (2^(3-1)*1000). The other branch of the D6 precedence rule.
Canonical 8-4-4-4-12 lowercase UUID — the form crypto.randomUUID() emits.
MASKING: emit is lowercase but the deployed UUID_RE carries /i, so uppercase validates. Two spellings of one id both pass; emitters MUST lowercase on emit (§8.1).
The grammar constrains neither version nor variant bits (§9), so the nil UUID passes. A workflow keyed on a nil correlation_id joins unrelated envelopes — recorded finding.
Concrete, wildcard-free mailbox — the form executeRequestReply mints and the guard accepts (§7.1).
not_now initial backoff delay in ms; delivery=1 is the first nak attempt (§4.1).
Deterministic doubling from JetStream deliveryCount: 2^(3-1)*1000 — stateless, survives consumer restarts (§4.1).

Backoff caps at 60s (§4.1). Rows beyond the consumer's configured max_deliver are unreachable, because JetStream counts every delivery regardless of nak reason — the S6 silent-loss context (§4.2, grill D14).
deliveryCount is clamped to >=1, so a caller passing 0 sees the initial delay rather than a 2^-1 underflow (§4.1).
compliance_block routes to dead-letter immediately (fast path) at any chain length — the same M7 policy would refuse on every peer, so retrying only burns budget (§5.1).

cant_do/wont_do accumulate; when the chain length (excluding not_now) reaches the consumer's configured exhaustion threshold (== max_deliver, the §4.2 equality invariant, grill D14; the reference configuration is 3) the task routes via exhaustion.
not_now NEVER routes to dead-letter — excluded from the exhaustion budget regardless of chain length (§5.1). This is precisely why the S6 silent-loss can occur at the JetStream layer below.
Below the exhaustion threshold no dead-letter route fires; the task is redelivered (§5.1).
